message:
chore(fcp-host,fwc): plumb capability_token: None through CancellationRequest call sites + rustfmt

Follow-up to the previous commit which added an optional CapabilityToken
field to fcp_kernel::CancellationRequest. Existing call sites (admin
cancel paths, internal helpers, unit tests, integration tests, the fwc
CLI client) do not participate in /rpc/cancel-self and should preserve
their pre-existing admin/operator-auth behavior, so they all explicitly
construct CancellationRequest with capability_token: None.

- crates/fcp-host/src/cancellation.rs: add capability_token: None to all
  17 CancellationRequest constructions inside the cancellation unit
  tests (cancel/double-cancel/serialization-roundtrip/checkpoint/audit
  variants). Behavior is unchanged because these tests exercise the
  CancellationController directly, which never inspects capability_token.

- crates/fcp-host/tests/host_connector_integration.rs: add
  capability_token: None to the three host-binary cancel-route
  integration tests (cancels_in_flight_invoke,
  allows_follow_up_invoke_after_cleanup,
  returns_too_late_for_completed_invoke). These exercise the admin
  /rpc/cancel route, so capability_token has no effect there.

- crates/fwc/src/main.rs: add capability_token: None inside
  cancel_dispatch's CancellationRequest construction. The fwc CLI is an
  admin/operator tool and routes to /rpc/cancel, so it stays on the
  admin auth path with no capability token.

- crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs: rustfmt-only reflow of long lines
  in verified_cancellation_principal, preflight_handler, simulate_handler,
  the cancellation principal-mismatch test assertion, the
  preflight_handler test, the cancel-self test token issuance, and the
  X-Principal spoofed-cancel test argument list. No semantic change.

Net effect: the new CancellationRequest field is now present at every
constructor in the workspace, the build/test surface is restored, and
formatting is back in line with rustfmt.
